details.summary: 14/18 claims have high evidence match (78.2% avg)

claim_verification_details.claim: Semaglutide (Wegovy) demonstrated mean weight loss of 14.9% over 68 weeks in the STEP 1 trial [1].
claim_verification_details.best_passage: At week 68, the mean change in body weight from baseline was −14.9% in the semaglutide group as compared with −2.4% with placebo...

claim_verification_details.claim: GLP-1 receptor agonists work by mimicking the incretin hormone, enhancing insulin secretion and reducing glucagon release [2].
claim_verification_details.best_passage: GLP-1 receptor agonists mimic the action of the endogenous incretin hormone glucagon-like peptide-1, which enhances glucose-dependent insulin secretion while suppressing glucagon release...

claim_verification_details.claim: Common side effects include nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, typically mild to moderate [3].
claim_verification_details.best_passage: The most common adverse events were gastrointestinal, including nausea (44%), diarrhea (30%), and vomiting (24%), most of which were mild to moderate in severity...

claim_verification_details.claim: Tirzepatide showed superior weight reduction compared to semaglutide in head-to-head trials [4].
claim_verification_details.best_passage: Tirzepatide was superior to semaglutide for the primary endpoint of mean percentage change in bodyweight from baseline to week 72...

claim_verification_details.claim: Long-term cardiovascular benefits have been demonstrated in SELECT trial [5].
claim_verification_details.best_passage: In the SELECT trial, semaglutide was associated with a 20% reduction in major adverse cardiovascular events over a median follow-up of 3.5 years...

claim_verification_details.claim: Patients should be monitored for signs of thyroid tumors as seen in rodent studies [6].
claim_verification_details.best_passage: In rodents, semaglutide causes dose-dependent and treatment-duration-dependent thyroid C-cell tumors at clinically relevant exposures...

claim_verification_details.claim: Insurance coverage has improved with FDA approval for obesity indication [7].
